chosen_action.title: Align distribution guard tests with normalized execution-board fingerprints

why_this_action.summary: The execution board still truthfully had no do-now packet before the 2026-05-26T13:14:38 short-window release, so the strongest same-run move was a concrete runtime repair that improves future lane-selection reliability without blurring measurement. I repaired the guard-state test harness to hash the execution board the same way the live selector does, preventing stale raw-board fixtures from masking real distribution-architecture regressions.
shared_findings_used: marketing_execution_board_latest.md: no truthful do-now packet exists in the current review window
distribution_lane_latest.json: current selector state is distribution_architecture_guard_pause under the active churn guard
adoption_metrics_latest.json: Codeberg remains flat, so fake-progress follow-through would not be a valid move

summary: Added a shared helper so guard-state fixtures use the selector's normalized execution-board fingerprint instead of raw markdown bytes.
Updated existing guard-pause and guard-follow-through fixtures to use the normalized fingerprint path.
Kept the live selector behavior unchanged after verification; only the stale test harness was repaired in this run.

expected_outcome.measurement_window: Next selector/code repair touching execution-board guard logic
expected_outcome.success_metric: Guard-state tests fail only on real selector regressions, not raw-vs-normalized fingerprint drift
expected_outcome.kill_condition: If a future empty-board regression still slips through despite the repaired fixture path, add a direct selector integration test for the failing fingerprint transition
